schema:propertyID.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability

schema:url.description: web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

ada.bbr.metadata.geochemProperties.instr.title: ADA Analysis Instrument
ada.bbr.metadata.geochemProperties.instr.description: ADA analytical instrument building block. Extends the core CDIF instrument building block with NeXus NXinstrument classification via additionalType. Instruments are typed as schema:Thing + schema:Product (from core) with domain-specific types (e.g. nxs:BaseClass/NXinstrument, ada:AMSInstrument) in additionalType.

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific instrument type classifications. Should include a NeXus base class (e.g. nxs:BaseClass/NXinstrument) and/or technique-specific identifier (e.g. ada:AMSInstrument, GCMD instrument identifier).

ada.bbr.metadata.geochemProperties.labor.title: ADA Analysis Laboratory
ada.bbr.metadata.geochemProperties.labor.description: ADA laboratory or facility building block. Extends the core CDIF spatialExtent (schema:Place) with NeXus NXsource classification via additionalType. Inherits place name, identifier, alternateName, geo coordinates, and geosparql geometry from spatialExtent.

schema:additionalType.description: Must include nxs:BaseClass/NXsource for NeXus facility classification.

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.pe.description: schema for cdif profile of schema.org/Person

schema:name.description: string label for person that is meaningful for human users, should format consistently. Recommend 'Family Name, Given name' format.

schema:alternateName.description: other labels by which the person might be known

schema:affiliation.description: if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.

schema:contactPoint.description: restrict to email only. Schema.org allows telephone and postal contacts as well

schema:name.description: string label for organization that is meaningful for human users

schema:alternateName.description: other labels by which the organization might be known

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.la.description: schema for a labeled link, a profile of schema.org/CreativeWork

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.mo.description: Funding acknowledgement properties, profile of schema.org/MonetaryGrant

@id.description: URI identifier for this funding record

schema:description.description: description of the funding or grant

@id.description: a identifier for an agent defined in this metadata, or externally; must be dereferenceable

cdif.bbr.metadata.qualityProperties.qual.title: definitions for simple quality measure property

dqv:isMeasurementOf.description: specify the quality measure that is reported, by name, with an ID ref, or as a Defined Term

@id.description: a resolvable reference to a representation of a quality measure

dqv:value.description: the reported result of the quality measure, either as a string or a defined term from a vocabulary

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.in.title: Instrument Description
cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.in.description: Schema for describing laboratory instruments and instrument systems. Supports ownership, manufacturer, model, commissioning dates, hierarchical sub-components via schema:hasPart, and domain-specific typing. Instruments may be standalone devices or complex systems with sub-components.

@id.description: Persistent identifier URI for this instrument metadata record.

@type.description: Must include schema:Product and schema:Thing. May include additional types.

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications. Should include a scientific instrument type URI (e.g. wd:Q3099911 for Wikidata scientific instrument).

schema:description.description: Text description of this instrument, its purpose, and capabilities.

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate name(s), e.g. specific make/model designation, common abbreviation.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier(s) for this instrument. Use PropertyValue for structured identifiers (e.g. PIDINST, serial number, inventory number).

schema:url.description: Landing page URL for this instrument.

schema:model.description: Product model information for this instrument.

schema:category.description: Instrument type classification(s) from a controlled vocabulary.

schema:contributor.description: Agents associated with this instrument in specific roles (e.g. operator, custodian, principal investigator). Use schema:owner for the owning organization.

schema:additionalProperty.description: Instrument-specific properties (measured variables, detection limits, calibration information, operating parameters, etc.).

schema:validThrough.description: Date the instrument was decommissioned (ISO 8601). Absent if still operational.

schema:hasPart.description: Sub-components of this instrument system. Each item is either an inline instrument description or an @id reference.

@id.description: Reference to a sub-component defined elsewhere.

schema:relatedLink.description: Links to related resources (manuals, datasheets, calibration records, related instruments).

schema:subjectOf.description: Catalog record metadata-about-metadata for this instrument description.

schema:dateModified.description: Date this metadata record was last modified (ISO 8601).

dcterms:conformsTo.description: Schema or profile this metadata record conforms to.

InstrumentComponent.description: A sub-component of an instrument system. Uses the same structure as the parent instrument but without nesting (single level of hasPart).

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.sp.description: Spatial Extent description for CDIF discovery profile.  Note required prefix declaration for geoSparql ( http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#) and OGC simple features (sf: http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#)

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type,laboratory classification, feature type).

schema:name.description: A place name for the location, either as a string or a DefinedTerm from a gazetteer.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.

schema:geo.description: Either a bounding box or a point location.

anyOf.description: A point location. Point locations are recommended for data that is associated with specific sample locations, particularly if these are widely spaced such that an enclosing bounding box would be a misleading representation of the spatial location. Be aware that some client applications might only index or display bounding box extents or a single point location.

A schema:GeoShape bounding box. The geometry is described with a set of latitude/longitude pairs (in that order).The documentation for schema:GeoShape states 'Either whitespace or commas can be used to separate latitude and longitude; whitespace should be used when writing a list of several such points.'

schema:box.description: A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.

anyOf.description: A schema:GeoShape curvilinear trace, for resource related to a linear trace like a ship track or airplane flight line

schema:line.description: A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

geosparql:hasGeometry.description: Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.

@type.description: MUST be sf: (<http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#>) SimpleFeature geometry type. See https://opengeospatial.github.io/ogc-geosparql/geosparql11/sf_geometries.ttl

@value.description: a WKT geometry description string, e.g. 'POINT(-76 -18)'

@id.description: identifier string for coordinate reference system, e.g., 'CRS84'

schema:propertyID.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability

schema:url.description: web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ

schema:name.description: string label for organization that is meaningful for human users

schema:alternateName.description: other labels by which the organization might be known

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.pe.description: schema for cdif profile of schema.org/Person

schema:name.description: string label for person that is meaningful for human users, should format consistently. Recommend 'Family Name, Given name' format.

schema:alternateName.description: other labels by which the person might be known

schema:affiliation.description: if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.

schema:contactPoint.description: restrict to email only. Schema.org allows telephone and postal contacts as well

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.ag.description: For more granularity on how a person contributed to a Dataset, use schema:Role. The schema.org documentation does not state that the Role type is an expected data type of author, creator and contributor, but that is addressed in this blog post (http://blog.schema.org/2014/06/introducing-role.html). see https://github.com/ESIPFed/science-on-schema.org/blob/develop/guides/Dataset.md#roles-of-people

@id.description: IRI reference to a Person or Organization defined elsewhere

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.ad.title: additionalProperty PropertyValue
cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.ad.description: PropertyValue values required to define a soft-typed property with a value.

schema:propertyID.description: identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.la.description: schema for a labeled link, a profile of schema.org/CreativeWork

title: Analytical Method Definition v3
description: A registered analytical method definition modeled as a cdi:Activity and schema:Action. The method identity (name, technique, instrument, laboratory) lives at the top level. The standard workflow is encoded in schema:actionProcess as a schema:HowTo containing an ordered sequence of cdi:Activity + schema:Action steps (sample preparation, calibration, acquisition, data processing, quality control). Each workflow step carries its own parameters, reagents, and instruments. Method-level parameters that apply across all steps remain at the top level in ada:methodParameters. Integrates Bioschemas vocabulary for computational tools and reagents, DDI-CDI for activity sequence, and dqv:hasQualityMeasurement for quality metrics.

@id.description: Persistent URI for this method definition in the registry.

@type.description: Must include cdi:Activity, schema:Action, ada:MethodDefinition, and bios:LabProtocol.

schema:name.description: Short descriptive name with version (e.g. "JEOL-8530F WDS Major Element Glass v2.1").

schema:identifier.description: DOI or other persistent ID for this method definition.

schema:description.description: Human-readable summary of the method, may be auto-generated from constant parameters at registration time.

schema:datePublished.description: ISO 8601 date when this method configuration was first used.

schema:dateModified.description: ISO 8601 date of last update to this definition.

schema:additionalType.description: Further classification of the activity type (e.g. schema:CreateAction for a production method).

schema:measurementTechnique.description: The analytical technique this method implements. Must be a DefinedTerm from a controlled vocabulary of techniques.

schema:object.description: Target material(s) this method is designed to analyse (e.g. silicate glass, olivine, spinel). Modeled as DefinedTerm(s) or free text.

schema:instrument.description: Primary instrument specification for this method. Use schema:hasPart for instrument sub-components (spectrometers, detectors).

bios:computationalTool.description: Software tools used for data acquisition, reduction, and processing. Each tool carries name, version, and optional URL.

bios:reagent.description: Reference materials, calibration standards, and chemical reagents used across this method. Reagents specific to a single workflow step should be placed on that step instead.

ada:laboratory.description: Laboratory where this method was developed. Optional; omit for methods that are instrument-generic.

schema:agent.description: Person or organisation who defined or is responsible for this method.

schema:actionProcess.description: The standard workflow for this method, expressed as a schema:HowTo containing an ordered sequence of workflow steps. Each step is a cdi:Activity + schema:Action describing a distinct phase (sample preparation, calibration, acquisition, data processing, quality control). Steps carry their own parameters, reagents, instruments, and sub-steps.

ada:methodParameters.description: Method-level parameters that apply across all workflow steps (e.g. method-scoped constants like matrix correction model). Step-specific parameters should be placed on the appropriate workflow step instead.

ada:analyteTemplate.description: Template for per-analyte (element-specific) parameters. Defines the columns that appear in the element table, each with scope, datatype, and constraints. Analyte rows become schema:variableMeasured entries in metadata records. Typically associated with the acquisition workflow step.

ada:defaultAnalytes.description: Pre-populated analyte rows for this method. Each entry is an object keyed by analyteColumn names.

dqv:hasQualityMeasurement.description: Quality measurements that characterize the method's expected performance (e.g. analytical precision, accuracy, counting statistics error). Uses the CDIF qualityMeasure building block.

schema:relatedLink.description: Publications, Zenodo records, or other references documenting this method.

WorkflowHowTo.description: The method's standard workflow expressed as a schema:HowTo. Contains an ordered array of workflow steps in schema:step, where each step is a cdi:Activity + schema:Action.

schema:name.description: Name of the workflow (e.g. "EPMA WDS analytical workflow").

schema:url.description: URL to a published protocol document.

schema:step.description: Ordered sequence of workflow steps. Each step is a cdi:Activity + schema:Action describing a distinct phase of the analytical procedure.

WorkflowStep.description: A single step in the analytical workflow, modeled as a cdi:Activity + schema:Action. Each step describes a distinct phase such as sample preparation, instrument calibration, data acquisition, data processing, or quality control. Steps carry their own parameters, instruments, reagents, and may contain sub-steps via schema:actionProcess.

schema:name.description: Name of this workflow step (e.g. "Sample preparation").

schema:description.description: Detailed description of what this step involves.

schema:position.description: Ordinal position in the workflow sequence (1-based).

schema:additionalType.description: Further classification of this step (e.g. bios:LabProcess for wet-lab steps, schema:CreateAction for production steps).

schema:instrument.description: Instrument or equipment used in this step.

bios:computationalTool.description: Software tools used in this step.

bios:reagent.description: Reference materials, standards, or reagents used in this step.

prov:used.description: Input entities consumed or referenced by this step (e.g. prepared sample from a prior step).

schema:result.description: Output entity produced by this step (can be referenced as input by a subsequent step).

schema:object.description: Reference to a prior step whose result is the input for this step (action chaining).

schema:additionalProperty.description: Step-specific parameters (e.g. beam conditions for the acquisition step, matrix correction settings for the data processing step).

ada:methodParameters.description: Step-specific method parameters with scope and tier metadata. These are parameters that belong to this particular workflow phase.

schema:actionProcess.description: Sub-workflow for this step, expressed as a nested schema:HowTo with its own ordered steps. Use for steps that have a multi-phase internal procedure (e.g. sample preparation with mount, grind, polish, coat).

dqv:hasQualityMeasurement.description: Quality measurements specific to this workflow step.

MethodParameter.description: A parameter specification for the method, typed as schema:PropertyValueSpecification. Uses schema.org properties for value constraints (defaultValue, minValue, maxValue, readonlyValue, valueRequired) and ada: extensions for analytical method semantics (fieldScope, category, tier). Link to a controlled vocabulary via schema:inDefinedTermSet.

schema:description.description: Guidance text shown to the user in the form.

schema:propertyID.description: URI identifying this parameter type in a formal vocabulary. Links to a skos:Concept or similar term definition.

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: SKOS ConceptScheme providing the controlled vocabulary for allowed values of this parameter. When present, form renders a dropdown/select populated from the vocabulary. Can be an object reference (@id URI), a labeled link (LinkRole with name and URL), or a plain URI string.

anyOf.description: Object reference to a vocabulary defined elsewhere.

schema:defaultValue.description: The default value for this parameter. For constant parameters (readonlyValue=true), this is the fixed value. For editable parameters, this is the pre-filled default.

schema:readonlyValue.description: true = constant, fixed for every session (read-only in form). false = editable (default or optional depending on valueRequired).

schema:valueRequired.description: true = must be provided (mandatory). false = optional. Combined with readonlyValue: readonly+required = constant mandatory; !readonly+required = editable mandatory; !readonly+!required = optional.

schema:minValue.description: Minimum allowed numeric value. Defines the lower bound of a valid range at the method level; specific values within this range may be set per element in the analyteTemplate.

schema:maxValue.description: Maximum allowed numeric value. Upper bound of valid range.

schema:stepValue.description: Granularity expected of the value (e.g. 0.1 for nA).

schema:valuePattern.description: Regex pattern for validating string values.

schema:multipleValues.description: Whether multiple values are allowed (default false).

ada:fieldScope.description: Whether this parameter is fixed at the method level, varies per analytical session, or is element-specific.

ada:category.description: Grouping label for form layout (e.g. "Beam Conditions", "Data Processing", "Quality Control"). String for simple cases; DefinedTerm to link to a controlled vocabulary of parameter categories.

ada:dataType.description: Expected data type for the parameter value.

ada:enumeration.description: Inline list of allowed values. Fallback when no SKOS vocabulary exists yet. If inDefinedTermSet is also present, this list is ignored in favour of the vocabulary.

schema:unitCode.description: URI for unit of measure (QUDT preferred).

ada:cdifPropertyPath.description: The CDIF building block property path this parameter maps to. Used for JSON-LD serialisation.

ada:tier.description: Mandatory / Recommended / Optional tier from the metadata profile. Informs validation strictness.

StepParameter.description: A schema:PropertyValue carrying a parameter setting for a workflow step. Simpler than MethodParameter — just name, value, and optional unit.

AnalyteColumn.description: Definition of one column in the per-analyte parameter table, typed as schema:PropertyValueSpecification. Each column defines what values are expected per element/oxide row. Analyte rows become schema:variableMeasured entries; columns become schema:additionalProperty within each.

schema:propertyID.description: URI identifying this column's parameter type in a formal vocabulary.

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: SKOS ConceptScheme providing the controlled vocabulary for allowed values in this column.

anyOf.description: Object reference to a vocabulary defined elsewhere.

schema:defaultValue.description: Default value for this column (pre-filled per row).

schema:readonlyValue.description: true = constant across all analytes (read-only column).

schema:valueRequired.description: true = must be provided for every analyte row.

ada:enumeration.description: Inline allowed values list (fallback when no SKOS vocabulary exists).

ada:cdifPropertyPath.description: How this column maps into the variableMeasured JSON-LD structure.

ComputationalTool.description: Software application used for data acquisition, reduction, or processing. Follows Bioschemas ComputationalTool pattern.

schema:name.description: Name of the software (e.g. "Probe for EPMA").

schema:description.description: Role or purpose of this tool in the method.

schema:url.description: URL to the software product or documentation.

ada:toolRole.description: Role this software plays in the analytical workflow.

Reagent.description: A reference material, calibration standard, or chemical reagent. Follows Bioschemas reagent pattern with extensions for geochemistry reference materials.

schema:name.description: Name of the material (e.g. "San Carlos olivine").

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier (IGSN, catalog number, GeoReM ID).

ada:reagentRole.description: Role of this material in the method.

schema:citation.description: Publication reference for the standard's accepted values.
